title: Thelypteris clivalis
intro: Thelypteris clivalis, also known as Cliff Fern or Mountain Fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and ability to thrive in humid, shaded environments. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions like arthritis and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Thelypteris clivalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris clivalis is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for the extraction of its bioactive compounds and utilization of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Thelypteris clivalis is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to Central and South America and is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Thelypteris clivalis places it within the genus Thelypteris and the order Polypodiales. This species is a part of the kingdom Plantae, further classified into the phylum Pteridophyta, and the division Pteridophyta. Thelypteris clivalis is a relatively rare species of fern, with limited information available on its classification and taxonomy.
